---
title: "Honors Class01 Activity: Of Dice and Men"
jupyter: ds313
lang: en
format:
  pdf:
    documentclass: article
    geometry: margin=1in
    fontsize: 11pt
    pdf-engine: pdflatex
    template-partials:
      - doc-class.tex
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        \usepackage{booktabs}
  html:
    toc: false
    embed-resources: true
    theme: default
---

::: {.content-visible when-format="html"}
**Team Members:** <input type="text" aria-label="Team member 1 name" style="width:16em; border:none; border-bottom:1px solid black;">

<input type="text" aria-label="Team member 2 name" style="width:16em; border:none; border-bottom:1px solid black;">
:::

::: {.content-visible when-format="pdf"}
**Team Members:** \underline{\hspace{7cm}}

\underline{\hspace{9cm}}
:::

Each team will receive a pair of dice. Roll the dice 50 times and record the total for each roll in the table on the back of this page along with the running grand total.

::: {.content-visible when-format="html"}

1. Grand total of all 50 rolls:
   <input type="text" aria-label="Grand total of all 50 rolls" style="width:8em; border:none; border-bottom:1px solid black;">

2. Divide the sum by 50 to calculate the average:
   <input type="text" aria-label="Average" style="width:8em; border:none; border-bottom:1px solid black;">

3. What is the expected value of the average? Explain.

   <textarea aria-label="Answer to question 3" rows="3" style="width:100%; border:1px solid #ccc; border-radius:3px;"></textarea>

4. How much does your average differ from this expected value?
   <input type="text" aria-label="Difference from expected value" style="width:6em; border:none; border-bottom:1px solid black;">

5. Do you think this difference is plausible given you only rolled the dice 50 times?

   <textarea aria-label="Answer to question 5" rows="3" style="width:100%; border:1px solid #ccc; border-radius:3px;"></textarea>

6. If you wanted to be very confident the dice were fair, how many rolls would you want to do? Explain your reasoning (even if it's not exact math).

   <textarea aria-label="Answer to question 6" rows="3" style="width:100%; border:1px solid #ccc; border-radius:3px;"></textarea>

7. The probability of rolling a 12 is 1/36. Out of 50 rolls, how many 12s would you expect on average?

   <textarea aria-label="Answer to question 7" rows="3" style="width:100%; border:1px solid #ccc; border-radius:3px;"></textarea>

8. How many 12s did you roll and does this seem to be in agreement with the odds?

   <textarea aria-label="Answer to question 8" rows="3" style="width:100%; border:1px solid #ccc; border-radius:3px;"></textarea>

9. Suppose a classmate claims your dice are unfair because you didn't get any 12s. What evidence would you use to argue for or against this claim?

   <textarea aria-label="Answer to question 9" rows="3" style="width:100%; border:1px solid #ccc; border-radius:3px;"></textarea>

10. Based on this activity, write one sentence that summarizes what you learned about randomness and averages.

    <textarea aria-label="Answer to question 10" rows="3" style="width:100%; border:1px solid #ccc; border-radius:3px;"></textarea>

:::

::: {.content-visible when-format="pdf"}

```{=latex}
\begin{enumerate}
\item Grand total of all 50 rolls: \underline{\hspace{5cm}}
\item Divide the sum by 50 to calculate the average: \underline{\hspace{4cm}}
\item What is the expected value of the average? Explain.\par\vspace{1cm}
\item How much does your average differ from this expected value? \underline{\hspace{3cm}}
\item Do you think this difference is plausible given you only rolled the dice 50 times?\par\vspace{1cm}
\item If you wanted to be very confident the dice were fair, how many rolls would you want to do? Explain your reasoning (even if it's not exact math).\par\vspace{1cm}
\item The probability of rolling a 12 is 1/36. Out of 50 rolls, how many 12s would you expect on average?\par\vspace{1cm}
\item How many 12s did you roll and does this seem to be in agreement with the odds?\par\vspace{1cm}
\item Suppose a classmate claims your dice are unfair because you didn't get any 12s. What evidence would you use to argue for or against this claim?\par\vspace{1cm}
\item Based on this activity, write one sentence that summarizes what you learned about randomness and averages.
\end{enumerate}
\vspace{1cm}
```

```{=latex}
\newpage
\renewcommand{\arraystretch}{1.8}
\begin{table}[h]
\centering
\caption{Dice Roll Data: Record the sum of each roll and the running total.}
\begin{tabular}{|r|c|c|r|c|c|}
\toprule
\textbf{Roll \#} & \textbf{Dice Sum} & \textbf{Running Total} &
\textbf{Roll \#} & \textbf{Dice Sum} & \textbf{Running Total} \\
\midrule
1  &  &  & 26 &  &  \\ \hline
2  &  &  & 27 &  &  \\ \hline
3  &  &  & 28 &  &  \\ \hline
4  &  &  & 29 &  &  \\ \hline
5  &  &  & 30 &  &  \\ \hline
6  &  &  & 31 &  &  \\ \hline
7  &  &  & 32 &  &  \\ \hline
8  &  &  & 33 &  &  \\ \hline
9  &  &  & 34 &  &  \\ \hline
10 &  &  & 35 &  &  \\ \hline
11 &  &  & 36 &  &  \\ \hline
12 &  &  & 37 &  &  \\ \hline
13 &  &  & 38 &  &  \\ \hline
14 &  &  & 39 &  &  \\ \hline
15 &  &  & 40 &  &  \\ \hline
16 &  &  & 41 &  &  \\ \hline
17 &  &  & 42 &  &  \\ \hline
18 &  &  & 43 &  &  \\ \hline
19 &  &  & 44 &  &  \\ \hline
20 &  &  & 45 &  &  \\ \hline
21 &  &  & 46 &  &  \\ \hline
22 &  &  & 47 &  &  \\ \hline
23 &  &  & 48 &  &  \\ \hline
24 &  &  & 49 &  &  \\ \hline
25 &  &  & 50 &  &  \\ \bottomrule
\end{tabular}
\end{table}
```

:::

::: {.content-visible when-format="html"}

### Dice Roll Data

Record the sum of each roll and the running total.

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis

cell = "border:1px solid #ccc; padding:4px;"
th = "border:1px solid #999; padding:6px; background:#f0f0f0; text-align:center;"

rows = "".join(
    f'<tr>'
    f'<td style="{cell} text-align:right;">{i}</td>'
    f'<td style="{cell}"><input type="text" aria-label="Dice sum for roll {i}" style="width:5em;"></td>'
    f'<td style="{cell}"><input type="text" aria-label="Running total after roll {i}" style="width:7em;"></td>'
    f'</tr>'
    for i in range(1, 51)
)

print(f"""<table style="border-collapse:collapse; width:100%;">
<caption style="font-weight:bold; margin-bottom:0.5em; caption-side:top;">
  Dice Roll Data: Record the sum of each roll and the running total.
</caption>
<thead>
<tr>
<th scope="col" style="{th} text-align:right;">Roll #</th>
<th scope="col" style="{th}">Dice Sum</th>
<th scope="col" style="{th}">Running Total</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>{rows}</tbody>
</table>""")
```

:::
